title: dryas punctata subsp. hookeriana
intro: Dryas punctata subsp. hookeriana, also known as the alpine rose, is a flowering plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest in North America. The medicinal properties of alpine rose are attributed to its leaves and stems, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, inflammation, and digestive problems. The health benefits associated with alpine rose include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce pain and promote healing, as well as its ability to support digestive health and reduce stress. Alpine rose can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to support overall health and well-being.
